howItWorks.intro: This 3d print time estimator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Layers = Object Height (mm) ÷ Layer Height
Path Length per Layer ≈ Shell Perimeter + (Infill Area ÷ Line Width)
Print Time ≈ Total Path Length ÷ Print Speed
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How accurate are 3D print time estimates?
faq.answer: Software estimates are typically within 10-20% of actual print time. Factors like acceleration, retraction, travel moves, and warm-up time can add to the total.

faq.question: Does layer height affect print time?
faq.answer: Yes, significantly. Halving the layer height (e.g., 0.2mm to 0.1mm) roughly doubles print time since twice as many layers are needed.

faq.question: What print speed should I use?
faq.answer: Most FDM printers work well at 40-60 mm/s. Faster speeds (80-100+ mm/s) are possible on CoreXY printers. Slower speeds (20-30 mm/s) improve quality for detailed prints.

faq.question: Is the 3D Print Time Estimator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard 3D printing and additive manufacturing formulas trusted by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the 3D Print Time Estimator?
sections.body: The 3D Print Time Estimator is a free online tool designed for makers and 3D printing enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the 3D printing and additive manufacturing space. By entering your print volume, layer height, print speed, you get instant results including total layers, estimated print time, est. filament used. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total layers right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In 3D printing and additive manufacturing, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your prints with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your print volume and need to find the right total layers. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Print Volume (cm³)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your print volume — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: layer height, print speed, infill percentage. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total layers, estimated print time, est. filament used. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your print volume before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total layers and estimated print time — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your prints.
If you're unsure about your infill percentage, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
